Throughout history, Iran loses almost every war it fights. But it just sticks around and keeps on coming.


Noah Smith 🐇🇺🇸🇺🇦🇹🇼 (@Noahpinion)

Partial list of people who conquered Iran:

1. Alexander

2. Trajan

3. Septimus Severus

4. Heraclius

5. Abu Bakr & co.

6. Toghril Beg

7. Hulagu

8. Tamerlane

But they did manage to hold off Saddam Hussein...

It was only a few years ago that I learned that some people are very insistent that you spell it “St. Paddy’s Day” and not “St. Patty’s Day” and I was so excited to have something new to be pedantic about.

Somehow, it was decided that 5th graders at my kid’s school should wear their class t-shirts today which are GRAY. 

Admittedly, my town is more Italian Catholic than Irish Catholic, but, like, every other little league coach is named Michael Somethingirish. So I can’t be the only one grumbling.
￼Finally threw out my Christmas tree!

If a character dynamic doesn’t work for you that’s fine. But that may actually mean this particular piece of entertainment isn’t right for you, not that you are morally or intellectually superior to it.

What’s that? You think Dwight is more sympathetic than Jim? You understand that everyone who made the show wanted you to feel the other way but you’re a crazy maverick who manages to feel the opposite? 

Sounds like you’re more interested in patting yourself on the back than enjoying a TV show.

Hey, here’s some unsolicited advice: Usually the best way to enjoy and engage with a piece of entertainment is not to try to outsmart it. 

Not liking the main character who is supposed to be sympathetic does not mean you have DEFEATED the work of art, nor should that be your goal.

I think not enough attention was payed to the passing of the last living Little Rascal this year. 
www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie...

Oh sure. When I was a kid, Perfect Strangers, Who’s the Boss, Growing Pains, Mr. Belvedere, Just the 10 of Us … all appointment viewing. But even then I knew they weren’t as good as Cheers or Newhart or early seasons of the Cosby Show (I didn’t know! I was 8!)

Would we count Wonder Years? That was a little before Roseanne and it was ABC’s last Emmy for Best Comedy until Modern Family two decades later. 

But, yes, ABC was pretty dedicated to pleasant, middle-of-the-road family fare like Who’s the Boss and Growing Pains.

For quite a while, but for some reason their US advertisements at the moment are all about why you don’t need to be afraid to shop there even though they’re weird and different. Which they are not. www.instagram.com/reel/DTbRDUZ...

I know we’re living in a undiscovered circle of hell at the moment, but this morning at Aldi I found a shopping cart that already had a quarter in it so after my shopping was done, guess who was walking out with 50 cents in his pocket!

I would like to remind everyone that in the 90s the Surgeon General suggested that it might be okay to tell children that masturbation exists and the country threw a tantrum so big she had to resign and we just didn’t have a Surgeon General for over three years.
This is definitely the repressed Irishman in me talking, but my worst nightmare would be hearing someone say that I was displaying “big feelings.”
Heck, I quoted “Her Majesty” in my Best Man toast at my brother’s wedding! (to be fair, it was kind of a joke reference because I had just quoted “The End” — you gotta love track-order humor)

On Valentine’s Day, the Wordle solution was BLOOM but apparently, if Wordlebot had it narrowed down to two options, it would have guessed GLOOM instead. 

Did Wordlebot recently go through a breakup?
